description: Everything identified in the 2026-05-21 dual-agent diagnosis that has not yet shipped: complete the Pro chat surface (per-experience AI follow-up, one shared orchestrator, fix dev-machine unconfigured test), make the map's data layer self-healing (auto-explore on empty filter, fix the 25km expand chain, Overpass category mapping, Foursquare fallback), let users configure categories (userTags schema, visible-categories multi-select, custom-tag pills), polish safe-area + tap-target visuals, and land a real dual-agent QA harness with screenshots (sc-evaluator, sc-executor, sc-loop). Source: docs/PRD/prd-post-pro-chat-rebuild-v1.md.

userStories.title: Make unconfigured-state orchestrator tests pass on dev machines
userStories.description: As a developer, I want testMissingKeyYieldsUnconfiguredState and testRepeatedStartWhileUnconfiguredStaysUnconfigured to pass even when GeneratedSecrets.deepSeekApiKey is non-empty (dev .env baked in), so CI parity is restored.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce an APIKeyResolver protocol (or equivalent seam) so tests can inject an empty-key resolver without depending on the build-time GeneratedSecrets value
VoiceAgentOrchestrator and AIService read the resolved key through that seam
testMissingKeyYieldsUnconfiguredState passes on a dev machine whose .env contains a real DeepSeek key
testRepeatedStartWhileUnconfiguredStaysUnconfigured passes under the same condition
No existing tests regress
xcodebuild build for iPhone 17 Pro / iOS 26.4 passes

userStories.title: Add VoiceAgentOrchestrator.rebindContext(_:) to swap experience scope without re-allocating
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one VoiceAgentOrchestrator instance to handle both the unscoped + button chat and per-experience chats, so memory stays bounded as users open many cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Add public func rebindContext(_ experience: Experience?) to VoiceAgentOrchestrator
rebindContext clears the current session and re-seeds the system prompt with (or without) the experience block — does NOT recreate AIService/VoiceService dependencies
Unit test asserts calling rebindContext(nil) then rebindContext(experienceA) then rebindContext(experienceB) keeps the same orchestrator instance and the latest system prompt matches the latest scope
xcodebuild build for iPhone 17 Pro / iOS 26.4 passes

userStories.title: Inject <experience_context> block into orchestrator system prompt builder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want buildSystemPrompt to optionally take an Experience and emit a <experience_context> block, so US-002 can pass scope without leaking coordinates.
acceptanceCriteria: buildSystemPrompt accepts an optional Experience parameter
When non-nil, the prompt contains a <experience_context> block with title, category (rawValue), cityCode, bestTimes summary, confidence.level, and soloScore overall
Coordinates (location.coordinates) are NEVER included in the block
Unit test asserts the block contents for a sample Experience and asserts no coordinate substring appears
xcodebuild build for iPhone 17 Pro / iOS 26.4 passes

userStories.title: Add 'Ask Solo about this' button on ExperienceDetailView
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to ask Solo follow-up questions about the experience I'm viewing, so I don't have to retype its name in the main chat.
acceptanceCriteria: ExperienceDetailView renders an 'Ask Solo about this' button below the hero card and above the 'Mark done' CTA
Button is hidden when aiService.isProTier == false AND Secrets.resolvedDeepSeekApiKey.isEmpty (mirrors the + button gate)
Tapping the button opens ChatSheet with the experience bound via rebindContext from US-002
Closing the sheet does NOT leak the experience context into the next unscoped + chat (rebindContext(nil) is called on dismiss)
Localized labels in en.lproj and zh-Hans.lproj under experience.askSolo.cta
XCTest case testExperienceAskAIInjectsContext asserts the system prompt contains <experience_context> with the experience's title
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot acceptance and downgraded XCUITest→XCTest to avoid simulator boot in Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Add userTags field to Experience schema with SwiftData migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Experience to support arbitrary user-defined tags alongside the category enum, so users can layer their own labels without weakening the enum.
acceptanceCriteria: Add userTags: [String]? (default nil; treated as []) to Models/Experience.swift
Mirror the field in packages/core/src/experience.ts (optional string array)
Add a SwiftData VersionedSchema migration that fills userTags = [] for existing rows; no data loss
Existing JSON seed files load without modification (field is optional)
pnpm parity:check passes
Unit test asserts encoding/decoding round-trips userTags through JSON
xcodebuild build passes

userStories.title: Add visibleCategories to UserPreferences and wire into FilterBarView
userStories.description: As a user, I want to hide categories I don't care about so the filter bar stays short.
acceptanceCriteria: Add visibleCategories: Set<ExperienceCategory> (default = all 8 cases) to UserPreferences
FilterBarView reads visibleCategories from UserPreferences via @Environment — no more hard-coded constant at FilterBarView.swift:30
When visibleCategories changes, FilterBarView re-renders to show only selected pills
Unit test asserts that removing .nightlife from visibleCategories drops the nightlife pill
xcodebuild build passes

userStories.title: Add 'Visible categories' multi-select row to SettingsView
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Settings UI to choose which built-in categories appear on the filter bar.
acceptanceCriteria: New row in SettingsView labeled 'Visible categories' under a 'Filter bar' group header
Tapping it pushes a list with a checkbox for each of the 8 built-in ExperienceCategory cases
Toggling a checkbox immediately updates UserPreferences.visibleCategories
Selection persists across app relaunch (UserPreferences persistence)
Localized strings in en.lproj and zh-Hans.lproj under settings.filter.visible_categories
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot/XCUITest acceptance to keep Ralph in build+unit-test scope (no simctl boot).

userStories.title: Add customTags to UserPreferences and render custom pills on FilterBarView
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define my own tag pills that filter experiences by Experience.userTags.
acceptanceCriteria: Add customTags: [String] (default []) to UserPreferences with persistence
FilterBarView renders one extra pill per entry in customTags, after the 8 built-in pills, using iconPill shape with icon 'tag.fill' and Color.accentColor
Tapping a custom pill filters visibleExperiences to those whose userTags contains the tag
Tapping the same custom pill again clears the filter (toggle behaviour matches built-in categories)
Unit test asserts a custom-tag tap filters to the matching experiences
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot/XCUITest acceptance to keep Ralph in build+unit-test scope (no simctl boot).

userStories.title: Add 'Custom tags' management screen to SettingsView
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Settings screen to add, rename, and delete my custom tags.
acceptanceCriteria: New row in SettingsView labeled 'Custom tags' under the 'Filter bar' group
Tapping it pushes a list where each row is one tag plus a + button to add and swipe-to-delete
Adding/deleting updates UserPreferences.customTags immediately and reflects in FilterBarView
Empty-tag input is rejected with a localized inline message
Localized strings in en.lproj and zh-Hans.lproj under settings.filter.custom_tags
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot/XCUITest acceptance to keep Ralph in build+unit-test scope (no simctl boot).

userStories.title: Add categoryToOverpassFilter mapping and accept category in OverpassService.fetchPOIs
userStories.description: As a user, I want category-tagged Explore results so tapping Coffee returns coffee shops, not a generic mix.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a static categoryToOverpassFilter: [ExperienceCategory: String] table in OverpassService — single source of truth

Mapping covers all 8 categories with sensible Overpass QL filters (e.g. amenity=cafe for .coffee, amenity=coworking_space for .work, leisure=park OR natural=* for .nature, tourism=attraction OR historic=* for .culture, amenity=restaurant for .food, leisure=spa OR healthcare=* for .wellness, amenity=bar OR amenity=nightclub for .nightlife, tourism=viewpoint for .hidden)
fetchPOIs gains an optional category: ExperienceCategory? parameter; when non-nil it injects the matching filter clause
Returned POIs round-trip into Experience.category matching the requested category
Unit test asserts the generated Overpass QL string for each of the 8 categories
Network-stubbed integration test confirms category round-trip
xcodebuild build passes

userStories.title: Auto-trigger exploreNearby when category filter yields zero results
userStories.description: As a user, I want the map to silently fetch more data when I tap a category and nothing shows up, instead of staring at an empty card.
acceptanceCriteria: When MapViewModel.applyFilters returns 0 visibleExperiences AND the user is inside a seeded city, schedule exploreNearby(at: exploreAnchorCoordinate, category:) after a 600 ms debounce
Trigger is gated by a per-category timestamp so the same category cannot fire more than once per 10 s
Free-tier users get a single-ring Overpass POI fetch; Pro users get the existing multi-ring schedule
If exploreNearby itself returns empty, fall through to the existing EmptyStateOverlay (no behaviour change in that branch)
Unit test asserts the per-category debounce window is respected (second tap within 10 s does NOT re-fire)
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot/XCUITest acceptance to keep Ralph in build+unit-test scope (no simctl boot).

userStories.title: Replace EmptyStateOverlay's static buttons with EmptyStateStage progression
userStories.description: As a user, when 5 km has no results, the expand button should actually do something, and if 25 km still empty, offer the next step rather than loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce enum EmptyStateStage { case tryExpand, tryExplore, browseCity } in MapViewModel
Stage transitions: tryExpand on first empty render; tryExplore after tapping expand still yields empty; browseCity after three consecutive empty cycles
tryExpand button text and action: 'Expand to 25 km' -> preferences.maxDistanceKm = 25 AND exploreNearby(at: anchor)
tryExplore button text and action: 'Explore farther' -> exploreNearby(radiusMeters: 12000)
browseCity button reuses existing 'Browse nearest city' behaviour
Three distinct localized strings (no shared 'no results' label)
Unit test drives the stage machine through tryExpand -> tryExplore -> browseCity
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot/XCUITest acceptance to keep Ralph in build+unit-test scope (no simctl boot).

userStories.title: Add FoursquareService as fallback data source after Overpass returns thin results
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to fall back to a second data source in cities where OSM is thin, so I don't see a dead-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Services/FoursquareService.swift mirroring OverpassService API: fetchPOIs(near:radiusMeters:category:) async throws -> [Experience]
Add Secrets.resolvedFoursquareKey (UserDefaults override -> GeneratedSecrets fallback, mirroring resolvedDeepSeekApiKey)
MapViewModel.exploreNearby: call Overpass first; if results.count < 5 AND resolvedFoursquareKey is non-empty, call Foursquare with the same params and merge
Merge dedupes by (latRounded4, lonRounded4) — keep the Overpass record when both sources match
Hard cap: at most 1 Foursquare call per exploreNearby invocation
Add daily counter UserPreferences.foursquareCallsToday for visibility (no enforcement in v1; reset at local midnight)
Unit tests cover the dedupe logic and the < 5 threshold gate
xcodebuild build passes

userStories.title: Safe-area audit across iPhone 17 family with screenshot evidence
userStories.description: As a user on iPhone 17 Pro/Pro Max/Air, no app control should overlap the status bar or Dynamic Island.
acceptanceCriteria: Take screenshots on three simulators (iPhone 17 Pro, iPhone 17 Pro Max, iPhone Air, all on latest iOS) showing the cold-launch map screen after granting when-in-use location
Save screenshots to docs/qa/safearea-audit/<device>-<iso-date>.png and reference them in this story's notes field upon completion
Each screenshot must show ALL of: city pill, filter bar, MapCompass, MapUserLocationButton, the + button, the explore button — none clipped by status bar / Dynamic Island / home indicator
If any device shows a clip, fix the underlying view's safe-area handling and re-screenshot
BottomInfoBar text is not clipped by the home indicator on any device
xcodebuild build passes

userStories.title: Enlarge city pill tap target to 44x44 pt minimum
userStories.description: As a user, I want the city pill to be easy to tap per Apple HIG.
acceptanceCriteria: City pill in MapOverlayView.cityPill uses .contentShape(Rectangle()) with an inset so the tappable frame is at least 44 pt on each axis even though the visual pill is smaller
XCTest hits the four corners of the 44x44 hit box and asserts the city picker sheet opens for each
Visual appearance of the pill is unchanged
xcodebuild build passes
userStories.notes: 2026-05-22: removed simulator-screenshot/XCUITest acceptance to keep Ralph in build+unit-test scope (no simctl boot).

userStories.title: Add sc-evaluator runtime: boot simulator, install build, run journey, write findings
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single shell command that boots the simulator, installs the latest SoloCompass.app, executes a named journey, and produces an append-only findings file.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/sc-evaluator/run.sh that takes a journey name as its first argument
run.sh boots an iPhone 17 Pro / iOS 26.4 simulator (or uses an already-booted one), installs the built .app, launches it, and steps through the journey
Exit code 0 = all assertions passed; exit code 1 = one or more findings raised
Findings written to scripts/sc-evaluator/findings/<run_id>.md where run_id is an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (e.g. 2026-05-21T19-45-00Z)
Findings file contains: journey name, per-step pass/fail, screenshot links, suggested fix anchors (file:line references)
Findings are append-only — re-running the same journey creates a new file, never overwrites
First journey shipped: home-screen-cold-start (placeholder that just launches and screenshots is acceptable for this story)
Skill definition file at ~/.claude/skills/sc-evaluator/SKILL.md that delegates to run.sh
Bash test invocation 'scripts/sc-evaluator/run.sh home-screen-cold-start' completes within 10 minutes on M-series hardware

userStories.title: Define journey YAML DSL and load/validate it
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a tiny YAML DSL to describe user journeys so authoring tests doesn't require reading harness internals.
acceptanceCriteria: Journeys live at scripts/sc-evaluator/journeys/<name>.yml
Supported step keywords: launch, tap, longPress, screenshot, assertVisible, assertText, wait
tap accepts either {x, y} coordinates OR {accessibilityId: "..."}
run.sh validates the YAML on load and fails fast with a clear error if an unknown step name or required arg is missing
Ship two journeys: home-screen-cold-start.yml and pro-chat-roundtrip.yml
Unit-style test: invoking run.sh with a journey containing an unknown step name exits non-zero and prints the offending step
DSL documented in scripts/sc-evaluator/SCHEMA.md

userStories.title: Implement screenshot step in sc-evaluator runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every journey to be able to call screenshot(label) so visual issues show up in findings.
acceptanceCriteria: screenshot DSL step takes a label argument
Each call saves a PNG to scripts/sc-evaluator/screenshots/<run_id>/<NN>-<label>.png via xcrun simctl io <udid> screenshot, where NN is a 2-digit zero-padded step counter
Each call appends a Markdown image link ![label](relative/path) to the active findings file under a ## Screenshots section
home-screen-cold-start.yml uses at least 3 screenshot steps (cold-launch, permission-granted, map-loaded)
scripts/sc-evaluator/screenshots/ added to .gitignore by default; opt-in via env var SC_EVALUATOR_KEEP_SCREENSHOTS=1 (the env var only changes git behaviour, not capture behaviour)

userStories.title: Define findings frontmatter schema and write JSON shadow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a stable, machine-parseable schema for the evaluator's findings so future tooling can consume them.
acceptanceCriteria: Every findings markdown file begins with a YAML frontmatter block containing: run_id (ISO-8601 UTC), journey, timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC), commit_sha (short, from `git rev-parse --short HEAD`), simulator (e.g. 'iPhone 17 Pro'), ios_version (e.g. '26.4')
Body sections in this order: ## Steps, ## Findings, ## Suggested Fixes, ## Screenshots
Each finding under ## Findings includes a file:line anchor where applicable
Alongside the markdown, write findings/<run_id>.json containing the same data in structured form
Schema (markdown + json shape) documented in scripts/sc-evaluator/SCHEMA.md
Add a JSON Schema file at scripts/sc-evaluator/findings.schema.json so the JSON shadow can be validated

userStories.title: Add sc-executor runtime: read latest findings, plan and emit patch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an executor that reads the most recent findings file and either prints a diff or commits a patch to a per-run branch.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/sc-executor/run.sh that reads scripts/sc-evaluator/findings/*.md and selects the most recent
Refuses to act if the latest findings file is older than 1 hour (prints a clear stale-input message and exits non-zero)
Default mode: dry-run — prints the proposed unified diff to stdout, no file changes
--apply mode: writes changes, commits to a branch agent/<run_id>, leaves it un-pushed
Always runs `xcodebuild build` after applying and aborts (resets the working tree) if the build fails
Skill definition file at ~/.claude/skills/sc-executor/SKILL.md that delegates to run.sh

userStories.title: Add sc-loop runtime that wires evaluator and executor with a hard iteration cap
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one command to drive the evaluator -> executor -> evaluator cycle until journeys pass or the iteration cap is hit.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/sc-loop/run.sh accepting <journey-name> and optional --max-iterations N (default 5, max 20 — values above 20 are clamped with a warning)
Each iteration logs to scripts/sc-loop/runs/<run_id>/iteration-<N>.md with timestamp, evaluator exit code, executor action
Loop terminates on: (a) evaluator exits 0 — success; (b) iteration cap reached — exhausted; (c) executor refuses to act (stale findings or build broken) — stopped
Final summary printed to stdout: total iterations, exit reason, path to the run directory
Skill definition file at ~/.claude/skills/sc-loop/SKILL.md that delegates to run.sh
Smoke test: running `scripts/sc-loop/run.sh home-screen-cold-start --max-iterations 1` on a clean checkout completes within 10 minutes and produces a runs/<run_id>/ directory
